title: Hardening: enforce Index.ValidatePath delegation in every path-accepting web handler

title: Chain A Fix: turn PoC green
description:
Chain A Fix — three options for refusing per-project pivot of server.custom_css (and other server-runtime keys). DECISION PENDING.

Option 1 (recommended): Refuse server-runtime keys from per-project sources entirely. In mergeProjectConfig, after unmarshalling the .fur.toml/.fur.yaml, zero out cfg.Server.*, cfg.Git.*, and any plugin-path keys. Per-project keeps theme/keymap/show_hidden/ignore/scroll_off. Smallest diff. Conservative. Matches existing bd memory per-project-fur-toml-fur-yaml-must-not. Also closes Chain L (FUR_* env override of the same keys) when the env merge layer applies the same filter.

Option 2: .fur.trusted allowlist in the user's global config. Once a repo absolute path is on the list, all keys in its per-project file are honored. More code; nicer UX for the legitimate 'my own repo' case. Filed separately as lookit-9py.3.5.3 (P2 placeholder).

Option 3: Interactive first-run prompt. Doesn't work for automation contexts (fur serve invoked by a watcher, scripts) so likely a non-starter standalone — could complement Option 2.

Recommendation: land Option 1 now; iterate to Option 2 if/when the friction is felt.

See bd memory server-custom-css-server-bind-server-port-and.
acceptance_criteria: PoC test passes. No regression in existing tests. Fix has minimal-scope diff. PR landed; bead closed with PR# in summary.
close_reason: PR #33: Chain A PoC + display/UX allowlist fix landed green on master

title: Chain M: slug collision -> anchor hijack -> content swap
description: Composes 4 facts about render.Slugify() + link graph: (1) headings coerced to slugs via many-to-one, (2) wikilink/anchor resolver picks SOME heading matching the slug, (3) broken-link detector greenlights if ANY heading resolves, (4) backlinks panel attributes by slug not source text. Attacker authors two headings slugging to same value; orders them so payload wins; legitimate-looking reference resolves to attacker content. Variants: homograph collision (Latin a vs Cyrillic а), cross-platform desync (fur slug vs GitHub slug), empty-slug collision, broken-link 'fix' trap. PoC: temp dir with two markdown files, assert wikilink resolution, then swap heading order in source and assert resolution changes. That order-controlled resolution is the bug. Relates to render.Slugify chokepoint.
close_reason: PR #42: Chain M — NFKC Slugify + centralized AnchorSlugs (TOC/API/TUI); supersedes #19

title: Chain L: env-var config pivot in shared environments

title: Stop publishing prebuilt binaries on GitHub releases (regression of lookit-bw2)
description:
**Regression of `lookit-bw2`.** That bd (closed 2026-04-16) had two halves: (1) delete existing releases/binaries, (2) rework `.github/workflows/release.yml` to drop the binary build+upload. Only half (1) was done. The next tag push — v1.0.1 on 2026-04-17, one day after `bw2` closed — re-published the full asset set: `fur-{darwin,linux}-{amd64,arm64}` + `checksums.txt`.

**Current state of GitHub releases (`Benjamin-Connelly/fur`):**
- v1.0.0 (2026-04-16) — no assets, post-bw2 clean state
- v1.0.1 (2026-04-17) — 5 assets back (the regression)

**Root cause:** `.github/workflows/release.yml` lines 22-42 still cross-compile for 4 targets, generate checksums, and `gh release create "$tag" ... fur-* checksums.txt`. Any future `v*` tag push will republish binaries.

**Distribution intent** per `lookit-n48` (closed): `go install github.com/Benjamin-Connelly/fur/cmd/fur@latest` from source. No prebuilt binaries until a Homebrew tap exists. User rationale: "no one is going to use other people's binaries" — trust + provenance concerns.

**Fix:**
1. Edit `.github/workflows/release.yml`: drop the cross-compile matrix (lines 22-31), the checksum generation (lines 32-33), and the `fur-* checksums.txt` arguments to `gh release create` (line 42). Keep: tests, release creation with `--generate-notes`.
2. Backfill: strip assets from v1.0.1. Either `gh release delete-asset Benjamin-Connelly/fur v1.0.1 <asset>` (5 times) or delete + recreate the release with notes only.
3. Verify next tag push produces a binary-free release.

**Acceptance:**
- Workflow no longer produces binaries on tag push
- v1.0.1 has zero assets (matching v1.0.0)
- README install instructions still reference `go install` only (already correct per `lookit-n48`)
close_reason: Closed

title: Single-file path argument opens entire directory instead of file
description:
Running `fur /path/to/single-file.md` opens the parent directory in the TUI file list rather than launching with that specific file as the active selection/preview.

**Repro:**
```
fur ./README.md
```
Observed: TUI launches in the parent directory; user has to fuzzy-filter or scroll to find README.md.
Expected: when arg resolves to a file (not a dir), launch with that file as the active selection AND its preview rendered.

**Likely entry points:**
- `cmd/fur/main.go` — root command path resolution (Cobra arg handling)
- `internal/tui/model.go` — initial model state; check whether it always treats the arg as a directory root
- `internal/index/index.go` `ValidatePath()` — may collapse single files to their parent dir

**Acceptance:**
- `fur ./some-file.md` opens TUI with some-file.md selected and previewed
- `fur .` and `fur /path/to/dir/` continue to behave as today (directory mode)
- Web mode (`fur serve ./some-file.md`) behavior decided in same PR: probably serve the parent dir but auto-route to the file at startup, or serve only that file — pick one and document
close_reason: Closed

value: Every security finding lands as a failing regression test before any fix. PoC PR breaks master; fix PR turns it green. Two beads, linked via bd dep add.

Per-project .fur.toml/.fur.yaml MUST NOT reach the plugin loader. Plugin hooks load only from ~/.config/fur/plugins/*.yaml (user-owned). Every PR touching the config loader preserves this — verified by regression test.
No network egress in tests. Everything runs in-process or under t.TempDir(). httptest.Server is fine; real DNS or sockets outside the test process are not.

Audit branch is audit/test-and-security. Top-level audit epic is lookit-9py. See docs/audit-prompt.md (NOTE: file does not yet exist; the spec was supplied to the session — Phase 0 should commit it under docs/ for future sessions to reference).
Cache dirs (~/.cache/fur/...) are 0700, files 0600, writes atomic via os.CreateTemp + os.Rename in the same dir to defeat symlink swap.
Index.ValidatePath() is the single shared path-security chokepoint. Every new file-serving path delegates to it. Never reimplement path containment inline.
render.Slugify() is the single source of truth for anchor slugs. Both TUI and web use it. Never reimplement slug generation.
